summary: PR #77904 is the canonical repair target, but it is not merge-ready: the hydrated artifact shows `check-lint` failed, comments were not hydrated despite 7 issue comments, mergeability is unknown, and Codex /review plus exact-head validation are missing. The PR is small, focused, maintainer-editable, and has successful behavior proof plus broad passing CI, so the correct path is to repair the contributor branch in place and then rerun external merge preflight.

repair_candidate.summary: Repair contributor PR #77904 in place so `openclaw models list` and `openclaw models status` terminate after successful one-shot output without breaking embedded/custom runtimes or tests.
repair_candidate.pr_title: fix(cli): exit after model inspection output
repair_candidate.pr_body:
## What Problem This Solves
`openclaw models list` and `openclaw models status` can print complete non-interactive output but keep the process alive through the route-first command path.

## Why This Change Was Made
The repair should preserve #77904's focused one-shot default-runtime exit behavior, clear the current lint failure, and keep embedded/custom runtime callers in control of their own exits.

## User Impact
Scripts and SSH diagnostics can rely on successful model inspection commands terminating after output is flushed.

## Evidence
Source PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/77904 by @dorukardahan. Required validation: focused CLI exit/model command tests plus `pnpm check:changed`, followed by Codex /review and exact-head merge preflight before merge.
credit_notes: Preserve @dorukardahan as the source contributor for https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/77904.
Keep the repair on the existing PR branch because maintainer_can_modify=true; if an executor later discovers the branch cannot be updated safely, replacement must carry explicit source PR credit.
